title: Uvaria kingii
taxonomy: Uvaria kingii is a species within the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This family encompasses over 2,000 species of flowering plants, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The Annonaceae family is further divided into 131 genera, with Uvaria being one of them. Uvaria kingii is a flowering plant native to Southeast Asia, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Annonaceae family. By understanding the classification of Uvaria kingii, scientists can better comprehend its characteristics and how it fits into the larger plant kingdom.
